title: 10 best herbal creams for cracked heels

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with cracked heels because of its unique properties that soothe, moisturize, and nourish dry skin. The cream's rich antioxidants and fatty acids deeply penetrate the skin to repair damaged tissues and restore natural moisture balance. The anti-inflammatory compounds in pot marigold also reduce redness and irritation, while its antimicrobial properties prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for healing. As a result, cracked heels become softer, smoother, and less prone to further cracking and dryness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream contains antioxidants like lutein and zeaxanthin, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin, promoting healing and soothing dry, cracked heels.

Emollient and moisturizing properties: The cream's emollient ingredients, such as fatty acids and triglycerides, provide long-lasting hydration to the skin, filling in cracks and crevices, and helping to soften and smooth rough patches.

Wound-healing and antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue strength, and inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Pot marigold roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with cracked heels.
Seeds: The seeds of the pot marigold plant contain fatty acids and antioxidants that help moisturize and soothe dry, cracked skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh calendula flowers and dry them completely in the shade for at least two weeks.
Infuse one cup of dried calendula flowers in one quart of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a strong decoction.
Combine four tablespoons of beeswax with eight ounces of coconut oil and heat in a double boiler until melted and smooth.
Gradually add the calendula decoction to the cooled wax-coconut oil mixture, stirring constantly until well combined.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized glass containers, label, and store in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with cracked heels because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active ingredients penetrate deep into the skin, soothing dryness and irritation caused by cracked heels. Additionally, tea tree oil's natural antifungal properties combat fungal infections that can exacerbate cracked heels, promoting healthy skin regeneration. As a result, tea tree creams help to soften, moisturize, and repair damaged heel skin, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with cracked heels.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Tea tree oil has potent antibacterial and antifungal compounds that help to prevent infections and promote healing in cracked heels.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in tea tree oil reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with cracked heels, allowing for a more comfortable healing process.

Emollient and moisturizing properties: Tea tree oil's emollient and moisturizing properties help to soften and hydrate dry, cracked skin, promoting healthy skin regeneration and reducing the appearance of cracks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and heal dry, cracked skin.
Barks: Contains terpenoids that have antifungal and antibacterial properties, making it effective against fungal and bacterial infections common on cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves, weighing 50g and chopping them finely for infusion.
Combine the chopped melaleuca alternifolia with 250ml of boiling distilled water, allowing it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Mix 20g of coconut oil with 40g of shea butter in a double boiler, heating until melted and blended smoothly.
Combine the strained melaleuca alternifolia infusion with the coconut-oil-shea-butter mixture, stirring well before pouring into sterilized jars for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with cracked heels because they provide intense moisturization, soothing dryness and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of chamomile help to reduce redness and swelling, while its antimicrobial properties prevent infection. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can exacerbate cracking. By applying a rich chamomile cream to the heels, you can lock in moisture and promote healing, leading to softer, smoother feet that are less prone to cracking.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with cracked heels.
Antimicrobial: Chamomile's antimicrobial property prevents bacterial and fungal infections from spreading in the affected area, promoting healing and reducing risk of complications.
Soilant and soothing: Chamomile's gentle, non-irritating properties help moisturize and soothe dry, cracked skin, promoting skin elasticity and flexibility.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, making them an excellent addition to creams for cracked heels.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have a soothing and calming effect on the skin, which helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and measure out 20 grams for the recipe.
Combine 100ml of sweet almond oil and 10g of beeswax in a heat-proof bowl and melt over low heat for 15 minutes.
Add the harvested dried matricaria chamomilla flowers to the melted mixture and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar and discard the solids after 1 hour.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before transferring to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with cracked heels because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herb's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, help to soothe and calm the dry, cracked skin on the heel area, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, St John's wort's antimicrobial properties aid in fighting off bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate the condition. As a result, the cream promotes healthy skin regeneration, leaving heels smooth, soft, and supple.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort contains antioxidants that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by dry skin, promoting healing and protecting against further damage.

Wound-healing properties: The flavonoids and hypericin in St John's Wort have been shown to accelerate wound closure and improve the strength of the skin, making it an effective treatment for cracked heels.
Anti-inflammatory effects: St John's Wort's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with cracked heels, creating a more comfortable healing environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the roots of St. John's Wort help soothe and calm dry, cracked heels by reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair.

Buds: The buds of St. John's Wort contain high levels of hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to promote skin regeneration and improve wound healing, making them an effective ingredient in creams for cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves, ensuring they are free of contaminants and pests.
Dry the collected plant material using shade drying or air circulation for at least three days.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of powdered hypericum perforatum with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil, steeping the mixture for four hours.
Filter the herbal infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding the solids and transferring the liquid to a clean container for further processing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with cracked heels because of its remarkable moisturizing properties. The cream's rich emollients and humectants deeply nourish and hydrate the dry, damaged skin on the feet, gently softening and soothing rough patches. Additionally, lavender's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds help to calm redness and irritation, promoting healthy healing and reducing the appearance of cracked heels. Regular use of this cream can leave your feet feeling smooth, supple, and utterly pampered.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: English lavender has antibacterial and antifungal properties that help to combat infections and prevent further infection of cracked heels, promoting healthy healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cracked heels, making them more comfortable and easier to manage.
Moisturizing properties: The fatty acids and vitamins in English lavender cream help to lock in moisture, soften dry skin, and promote the natural healing process of cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain a high concentration of essential oils, particularly linalyl acetate and linalool, which help to soothe and calm dry, cracked skin.
Leaves: The leaves of English lavender are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which work to protect the skin from further damage and promote healing of cracks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried lavender flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered lavender with 100ml of sweet almond oil and stir until well combined for 5 minutes.
Add 20% beeswax by weight to the mixture and heat it in a double boiler until melted for 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches 60°c before whipping with an electric whisk for 5 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and seal them while still warm, allowing them to cool completely before storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wheat creams helps with cracked heels because they provide an intense moisturizing treatment that deeply nourishes and softens dry, rough skin. The natural oils and antioxidants in wheat help to repair and regenerate damaged cells, reducing the appearance of cracks and fissures. Additionally, the emollient properties of wheat cream lock in moisture, leaving heels feeling smooth, supple, and protected. Regular use can also help to prevent future cracking by maintaining a healthy moisture barrier on the skin's surface.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal wheat creams containing anti-inflammatory herbs like tea tree oil, chamomile, or lavender can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with cracked heels, allowing for faster healing.

Moisturizing properties: Ingredients like shea butter, coconut oil, or olive oil in herbal wheat creams provide long-lasting moisture to dry, cracked skin, promoting healthy skin cell growth and reducing the appearance of cracks.

Antimicrobial properties: Herbs like neem, calendula, or eucalyptus in herbal wheat creams have antibacterial and antifungal properties that help prevent infection and promote wound healing by eliminating bacteria and fungi that can aggravate cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, wheat roots help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Bark: Containing salicylic acid, a natural exfoliant, wheat bark helps remove dead skin cells and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried wheat grass and grind it into fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the ground wheat grass powder with 250ml of distilled water in a saucepan and simmer for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Add 20g of beeswax pellets and 10g of coconut oil to the saucepan and stir until melted completely after 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before whipping the mixture with an electric mixer for 2 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and store in a cool dry place for up to 6 weeks or use immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with cracked heels because of its unique ability to soothe and moisturize dry, rough skin. The cream's astringent properties help to shrink the size of pores, reducing flaking and peeling, while its emollient properties lock in moisture to soften and hydrate the heel. Additionally, witch hazel has natural anti-inflammatory properties that calm redness and irritation, leaving heels looking smoother and healthier. Regular use can effectively repair and rejuvenate cracked heels, revealing a softer, more radiant appearance.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Witch Hazel's astringent properties help to reduce moisture and shrink the appearance of pores, which can aid in drying out and reducing the size of cracked heels.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Witch Hazel has anti-inflammatory compounds like tannins and flavonoids that can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with cracked heels.

Antimicrobial properties: Witch Hazel's antimicrobial agents, such as gallic acid and salicin, have antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help prevent infection and promote healthy wound healing in cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in soothing and calming irritated skin.
Buds: Containing high levels of salicin, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried hamamelis virginiana bark in hot water for 10 minutes to extract its medicinal properties.
Combine the infused liquid with coconut oil and beeswax, melting the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 45 minutes before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer.
Add vitamin e oil and shea butter, stirring well to combine ingredients and ensure even distribution of dosages.
Pour the final cream into sterilized containers, allowing it to set at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with cracked heels because of its natural emollient properties that deeply nourish and moisturize dry, rough skin. The cream's rich antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm irritated areas, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, dandelion's gentle exfoliating properties help to remove dead skin cells, revealing smoother, healthier-looking heels. As a result, cracked heels are gradually repaired, leaving them feeling soft, supple, and looking radiant.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cracked heels, promoting a more comfortable healing process.
Moisturizing properties: The emollient and humectant properties of dandelion cream help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked skin, reducing the appearance of cracks and fissures on the heel.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant compounds protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and cracking on the heels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are rich in vitamins and minerals, which help to nourish and moisturize dry, cracked skin.
Fruits: The fruits of the dandelion plant contain antioxidants that soothe and calm irritated skin, while also promoting collagen production.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality taraxacum officinale roots dried to preserve potency and crush them into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine one part crushed taraxacum officinale roots with two parts coconut oil in a clean glass bowl and let it steep for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another bowl, discarding solids and retaining infused coconut oil.
Mix the infused coconut oil with 10% beeswax pellets and 5% vitamin e oil in a small saucepan over low heat stirring occasionally until melted and combined.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars filling to the brim and seal immediately allowing it to set at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with cracked heels because they contain soothing and moisturizing properties that calm dry, irritated skin. The anti-inflammatory compounds in plantain also reduce redness and swelling, allowing the skin to heal faster. As the cream is absorbed into the skin, it provides a protective barrier that locks in moisture and shields against environmental stressors, promoting healthy skin regeneration and reducing the appearance of cracks and fissures.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Plantain creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with cracked heels, allowing for faster healing and relief.

Antibacterial and antifungal property: Plantain creams have antibacterial and antifungal properties that prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healthy wound healing.

Moisturizing and emollient property: Plantain creams are rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as fatty acids, which help lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked skin, providing long-lasting relief from discomfort and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the plantain plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and repairing cracked skin.
Barks: The barks of the plantain plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it an ideal ingredient for treating cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried plantago major leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within an hour of harvesting.
Mix the ground plantago major leaves with olive oil at a ratio of one part herb to two parts oil in a clean glass jar for 24 hours.
Add beeswax pellets to the herbal infusion mixture in a ratio of one part wax to two parts infusion and stir until dissolved over a low heat within an hour.
Remove from heat and let cool until it reaches a thick paste consistency then whip with a hand mixer or spatula for five minutes to increase emollience properties within 15 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars filling them to the brim and seal tightly within one day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with cracked heels because they provide a rich source of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that nourish and moisturize dry, damaged skin. The anti-inflammatory properties of nettle cream reduce redness and swelling, while its natural antibacterial compounds prevent infection and promote healing. Additionally, the cream's emollient properties fill in gaps between skin cells, providing an extra layer of protection against further damage, leaving heels soft, smooth, and healthy-looking.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle cream reduces inflammation and swelling associated with cracked heels, promoting healing and soothing discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antifungal properties in stinging nettle cream help to prevent infection and promote the growth of healthy skin by controlling the spread of harmful bacteria and fungi.
Moisturizing and emollient properties: Stinging nettle cream's moisturizing and emollient properties help to lock in moisture, soften and hydrate dry, cracked skin, and reduce roughness and scaling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Stinging nettle roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Barks: The barks of the stinging nettle plant contain astringent properties that aid in drying up excess moisture, making it beneficial for cracked heel treatment.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of fresh or dried stinging nettle leaves urtica dioica and 1 cup of coconut oil.
Combine the nettle leaves with 4 cups of water in a large pot and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil with the infused nettle liquid and stir until melted.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and let cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with cracked heels because of its unique blend of herbal ingredients that provide intense moisturization and nourishment. The cream's rich antioxidants and emollients penetrate deep into the skin, repairing damaged tissue and hydrating dry, rough patches. Additionally, the Tasmanian blue gum extract itself has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritation and discomfort, promoting healing and flexibility in even the most stubborn cases of cracked heels.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cracked heels.

Antimicrobial properties: The essential oils present in the cream, such as eucalyptol and globulol, inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infections and worsen cracked heel symptoms.
Moisturizing and emollient properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's natural waxes and oils help to lock in moisture, soothe dry skin, and provide a protective barrier against further drying and cracking.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm irritated skin on cracked heels.
Barks: Containing natural astringents, barks aid in tightening and firming up dry, cracked skin.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground eucalyptus powder with 100g of coconut oil and 50g of beeswax in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a makeshift one created by a stainless steel bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Stir occasionally for 30 minutes to melt the beeswax and mix well, then remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Whip the cooled mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens into a smooth cream, taking approximately 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry creams helps with cracked heels because of its rich emollient properties that deeply moisturize and nourish dry, rough skin. The cream's active ingredients, such as berberine and other plant extracts, work together to soften and soothe cracked heels, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy cell growth. As a result, the skin becomes smoother, softer, and more resilient, reducing the appearance of cracks and fissures.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Barberry creams contain berberine, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with cracked heels, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antimicrobial properties: The cream's antimicrobial properties help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can exacerbate cracked heels, promoting healthy skin regeneration and preventing further damage.

Exfoliating and moisturizing properties: Barberry creams contain emollients and humectants that gently exfoliate dead skin cells, while also moisturizing and hydrating the skin, helping to soften and soothe dry, cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to soften and moisturize dry skin, making them effective for treating cracked heels.
Buds: Containing high amounts of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe and calm irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh berberis vulgaris leaves and stems in the early morning dew.
Dry the harvested plant material for at least two hours under shade or in a food dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for one hour.
Grind 100 grams of dried berberis vulgaris into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground berberis vulgaris with 200 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar and let it cool before using as a cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with cracked heels because of its unique combination of soothing and moisturizing properties. The licorice root extract in the cream has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with cracked heels. Additionally, the cream's emollient ingredients deeply nourish and hydrate the skin, filling in cracks and crevices to provide long-lasting relief from discomfort and dryness. As a result, herbal licorice creams offer effective and natural treatment for dry, cracked heels.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps to soothe and calm dry, irritated skin by reducing inflammation and promoting hydration, making it ideal for relieving dryness and cracking on the feet.
Licoric acid: Licoric acid has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to prevent infections and reduce the risk of further irritation or damage to the cracked heel area.
Saponins: Saponins have emollient and moisturizing properties that help to soften and hydrate the skin, reducing dryness and cracking, and promoting healthy skin cell turnover.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help relieve dryness and itching associated with cracked heels.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and saponins that exhibit moisturizing and protective effects, making them useful for treating dry and damaged skin on the feet.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and grind 250g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground glycyrrhiza glabra powder with 100ml of coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over simmering water and stir until the mixture reaches a temperature of 60-70°c.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it steep for 30 minutes to allow the herbal extract to infuse into the coconut oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discard the solids, and let the cream cool before refrigerating it for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with cracked heels because of its unique blend of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals. The cream's active ingredients, including allantoin and rosmarinic acid, work together to promote skin regeneration and improve tissue elasticity. This helps to soften and repair dry, cracked skin on the heels, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy, smooth appearance. As a result, comfrey creams provide effective relief for individuals suffering from dry, cracked heels, leaving their skin feeling soft, supple, and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Comfrey creams contain allantoin, a natural humectant that helps to lock in moisture and promote skin regeneration, reducing the appearance of cracks and fissures on the heel.
Tannins: The tannins present in comfrey cream have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with cracked heels, making it easier for the skin to heal.
Allyl sulfides: Comfrey creams contain allyl sulfides, which have antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infection and promote a healthy healing environment for the skin on the heel.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots contain a high concentration of allantoin, a natural compound that promotes skin regeneration and helps to repair dry, cracked heels.
Buds: Comfrey buds are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated skin, making them ideal for soothing dry, cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh symphytum officinale leaves and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the leaves in a warm oven at 60°c for 30 minutes to preserve their bioactive compounds.
Combine 50g of dried symphytum officinale leaves with 250ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass container for 2 weeks, shaking daily, to allow for infusion.
Filter the infused oil through cheesecloth and combine it with 200g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter to create a creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet violet creams helps with cracked heels because of its exceptional moisturizing properties. The cream's rich blend of natural oils, including coconut oil and shea butter, deeply hydrates and nourishes dry, cracked skin on the heels. Additionally, sweet violet extract has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities that help soothe irritation, reduce redness, and promote healing. Moisturizing properties: The emollient and humectant properties of sweet violet cream help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked skin, promoting healthy hydration and barrier function.

Antimicrobial properties: Sweet violet's antimicrobial compounds, such as terpenes and phenolic acids, help to prevent infection and promote wound healing by controlling the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming dry, cracked skin.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide moisture to dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest viola odorata leaves and flowers and dry them properly in shade for 7-10 days.
Mix dried herbs with beeswax pellets, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture until beeswax melts completely and blend well to create a smooth consistency.
Add vitamin e oil and aloe vera gel to the mixture and stir well for 5 minutes before pouring into jars.
Allow the cream to cool down, set, and mature for at least 24 hours before applying on cracked heels.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with cracked heels because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's natural ingredients, such as lemon balm extract and glycerin, work together to calm and moisturize dry, cracked skin. As a result, the cream helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing, filling in cracks and crevices to leave feet feeling soft, smooth, and nourished. Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm cream can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cracked heels, promoting faster healing and soothing discomfort.

Moisturizing properties: Lemon balm's emollient properties help to lock in moisture, soften and hydrate dry skin, and promote the formation of a protective barrier that shields the skin from further damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in soothing and treating dry, cracked skin.
Buds: Lemon balm buds contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation, making them suitable for creams aimed at repairing damaged skin on heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in full bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the plant material in a warm, dark place for seven days to reduce moisture.
Combine one part dried melissa officinalis with four parts sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes or until the beeswax melts completely incorporating all ingredients smoothly.
Strain the cooled cream through cheesecloth into sterilized jars for long shelf life storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers creams helps with cracked heels because of its unique ability to deeply moisturize and nourish dry, rough skin. The herbal extract in cleavers cream works wonders by locking in moisture, reducing inflammation, and promoting cell regeneration. This results in a significant reduction in the appearance of cracks and fissures, leaving heels soft, smooth, and supple. Regular use of cleavers creams can also help to prevent future cracking by maintaining healthy skin hydration levels.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Cleavers cream reduces inflammation and swelling in cracked heels, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Cleavers cream's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and slow down the healing process, keeping cracked heels soft and supple.

Wound-healing properties: Cleavers cream contains compounds that stimulate collagen production, improve blood circulation, and promote cellular regeneration, helping to accelerate the repair of cracked heels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to soften and moisturize dry skin, making them effective for soothing cracked heels.
Barks: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and pain associated with cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh galium aparine leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind 100g of dried galium aparine into a fine powder using an electric grinder.
Mix the powdered galium aparine with 250ml of coconut oil and 50ml of beeswax in a double boiler until melted.
Remove from heat and let cool for 30 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer to desired consistency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for cracked heels are topical treatments infused with natural herbs and extracts that work wonders to soothe and heal dry, cracked skin. These creams help by providing intense moisture, reducing inflammation, and promoting collagen production, which in turn reduces the appearance of cracks and fissures on the heels. Examples include creams containing aloe vera, vitamin E, and tea tree oil, which have been shown to improve skin elasticity and reduce discomfort. By using herbal creams for cracked heels, individuals can enjoy softer, healthier-looking skin, reducing embarrassment and improving overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat cracked heels with herbal creams is by applying a thick layer of cream containing soothing herbs like Aloe vera, Coconut oil, or Calendula on the affected area. Leave it overnight and wash off in the morning. Repeat this process regularly to lock in moisture and promote healing. Additionally, exfoliate gently once a week to remove dead skin cells, allowing for better absorption of the herbal cream.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cracked heels that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, psoriasis, and dry skin conditions. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and coconut oil help to soothe and moisturize the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. These creams can also be used to treat conditions such as keratosis pilaris, which causes rough patches on the arms and legs.
